title: Publish docs as VitePress site on GitHub Pages
description:
**Size:** 🔧 small
**Category:** docs
**Priority:** medium
**Source:** Proposed for 'The documentation under docs/ should be analogous to https'

Create a VitePress documentation site for the forge repository and deploy it to GitHub Pages. Involves 7 file operations:

1. **`package.json`** (root) — `name: "c5c3-forge-docs"`, VitePress `^1.6.3` devDependency, scripts: `docs:dev`/`docs:build`/`docs:preview`
2. **`docs/.vitepress/config.ts`** — Site config with `title: "CobaltCore Forge"`, `base: '/forge/'`, sidebar linking 5 existing reference docs (`keystone-crd`, `ci-workflow`, `build-images-workflow`, `container-images`, `kubernetes-packages`), GitHub social link to `c5c3/forge`
3. **`docs/.vitepress/theme/index.ts`** — Re-export DefaultTheme with custom.css import (identical to C5C3/C5C3 pattern)
4. **`docs/.vitepress/theme/custom.css`** — 1440px max-width, code block horizontal scroll, table overflow handling, sidebar width adjustments (identical to C5C3/C5C3)
5. **`docs/index.md`** — Landing page with hero section, project overview, link to architecture docs at `c5c3.github.io/C5C3`
6. **`.github/workflows/deploy-docs.yaml`** — Two-job workflow (build → deploy) following `github-actions-workflow-spdx-header-and-structure-convention` pattern exactly: SPDX header, quoted `"on"`, `permissions` block (contents: read, pages: write, id-token: write), concurrency group with cancel-in-progress on PR only. Triggers on push to main (paths: docs/**, package.json, package-lock.json, workflow file) + workflow_dispatch. Uses actions/checkout@v4, setup-node@v4 (Node 22), configure-pages@v5, upload-pages-artifact@v3, deploy-pages@v4
7. **`.gitignore`** (edit) — Add `node_modules/`, `docs/.vitepress/cache/`, `docs/.vitepress/dist/`

All new files must carry SPDX Apache-2.0 headers per `spdx-apache-20-header-on-infrastructure-files` pattern. Workflow must use `.yaml` extension per forge convention.

**Rationale:** The 5 reference docs under docs/reference/ are only accessible via GitHub's file browser, which lacks navigation, search, and readable formatting. Publishing as a VitePress site provides a professional documentation experience at c5c3.github.io/forge with sidebar navigation, consistent styling, and automatic deployment on push to main — matching the existing C5C3/C5C3 architecture docs site pattern.

**Affected Areas:**
- package.json
- docs/.vitepress/config.ts
- docs/.vitepress/theme/index.ts
- docs/.vitepress/theme/custom.css
- docs/index.md
- .github/workflows/deploy-docs.yaml
- .gitignore

stories.title: Developer views forge documentation as a professional site
stories.want: to browse the forge reference documentation on a VitePress-powered site with sidebar navigation
stories.so_that: I can quickly find and read reference docs without navigating GitHub's file browser
criteria: Site is accessible at c5c3.github.io/forge
Sidebar lists all 5 reference docs: keystone-crd, ci-workflow, build-images-workflow, container-images, kubernetes-packages
Landing page (index.md) shows hero section with project name and description
Landing page links to architecture docs at c5c3.github.io/C5C3

stories.title: Documentation auto-deploys on push to main
stories.want: documentation changes pushed to main to automatically build and deploy to GitHub Pages
stories.so_that: the published site stays in sync with the repository without manual intervention
criteria: Workflow triggers on push to main when docs/**, package.json, package-lock.json, or the workflow file change
Workflow also supports manual trigger via workflow_dispatch
Build job runs npm ci and npm run docs:build
Deploy job publishes to GitHub Pages after successful build

stories.title: Developer runs docs site locally for preview
stories.want: to run the documentation site locally with hot reload
stories.so_that: I can preview changes before pushing
criteria: npm run docs:dev starts local dev server
npm run docs:build produces static output in docs/.vitepress/dist/
npm run docs:preview serves the built output locally
package.json has VitePress ^1.6.3 as devDependency

stories.title: Site styling matches the C5C3 architecture docs pattern
stories.want: the forge docs site to have the same wide-content styling as the C5C3 architecture docs
stories.so_that: code blocks, tables, and ASCII diagrams render properly across both sites
criteria: Content area max-width is 1440px
Code blocks have horizontal scroll for overflow
Tables have overflow-x: auto for wide tables
Sidebar width is 280px (300px at >= 1440px viewport)

stories.title: Repository ignores VitePress build artifacts
stories.want: node_modules/, VitePress cache, and dist directories to be gitignored
stories.so_that: build artifacts and dependencies are not committed to the repository
criteria: .gitignore contains node_modules/
.gitignore contains docs/.vitepress/cache/
.gitignore contains docs/.vitepress/dist/

stories.title: All new files carry SPDX license headers
stories.want: all new files to have proper SPDX Apache-2.0 license headers
stories.so_that: the repository maintains consistent license compliance
criteria: TypeScript files use // SPDX-FileCopyrightText and // SPDX-License-Identifier comments
CSS files use /* SPDX-FileCopyrightText */ and /* SPDX-License-Identifier */ comments
YAML workflow file uses # SPDX-FileCopyrightText and # SPDX-License-Identifier comments
All headers reference 'Copyright 2026 SAP SE or an SAP affiliate company' and 'Apache-2.0'

requirements.description: The repository SHALL have a root-level package.json with VitePress ^1.6.3 as devDependency and docs:dev, docs:build, docs:preview scripts
requirements.rationale: Provides the build tooling for the documentation site, matching the C5C3/C5C3 package.json structure

scenarios.name: package.json is valid Node.js project config
scenarios.when: npm install is run in the repository root
scenarios.then: VitePress is installed as a devDependency

scenarios.name: docs:build script produces static output
scenarios.then: VitePress builds the docs/ directory into docs/.vitepress/dist/
and_then: dist/ contains index.html and asset files

scenarios.name: package.json has correct project name
scenarios.then: the name field is 'c5c3-forge-docs'

requirements.description: The VitePress config SHALL define site title 'CobaltCore Forge', base '/forge/', sidebar with all 5 reference docs, and GitHub social link to c5c3/forge
requirements.rationale: Provides proper site metadata, navigation, and GitHub link for the documentation site

scenarios.name: Site title is correct
scenarios.then: the HTML title contains 'CobaltCore Forge'

scenarios.name: Base path is /forge/
scenarios.when: the site is deployed to GitHub Pages
scenarios.then: all asset URLs are prefixed with /forge/
and_then: the site is accessible at c5c3.github.io/forge

scenarios.name: Sidebar lists all reference docs
scenarios.then: it contains links to keystone-crd, ci-workflow, build-images-workflow, container-images, and kubernetes-packages
and_then: each link resolves to the correct markdown file under docs/reference/

scenarios.name: GitHub social link is present
scenarios.then: a GitHub icon links to https://github.com/c5c3/forge

requirements.description: The theme SHALL re-export DefaultTheme with custom.css import, identical to the C5C3/C5C3 pattern
requirements.rationale: Ensures consistent styling between the forge docs and the architecture docs site

scenarios.name: Theme re-exports DefaultTheme
scenarios.then: it exports DefaultTheme as the default export
and_then: custom.css is imported as a side effect

scenarios.name: Custom CSS sets 1440px max-width
scenarios.then: the --vp-layout-max-width CSS variable is set to 1440px

scenarios.name: Code blocks have horizontal scroll
scenarios.when: a code block exceeds the container width
scenarios.then: overflow-x: auto is applied to the pre element

requirements.description: The docs/index.md SHALL contain a VitePress hero section with project overview and link to architecture docs
requirements.rationale: Provides a landing page for the documentation site with context and navigation to related resources

scenarios.name: Landing page has hero section
scenarios.then: a hero section is displayed with project name and tagline
and_then: an action button links to the reference documentation

scenarios.name: Landing page links to architecture docs
scenarios.then: a link to c5c3.github.io/C5C3 is present
and_then: the link text clearly identifies it as architecture documentation

scenarios.name: Landing page provides project overview
scenarios.then: a brief description of the forge repository purpose is shown

requirements.description: The GitHub Actions workflow SHALL follow the github-actions-workflow-spdx-header-and-structure-convention pattern with two jobs (build, deploy) and correct triggers
requirements.rationale: Ensures the workflow matches repository conventions and deploys docs automatically

scenarios.name: Workflow has SPDX header and correct structure
scenarios.then: it starts with SPDX Apache-2.0 header, uses quoted 'on', and has permissions block
and_then: permissions include contents: read, pages: write, id-token: write

scenarios.name: Workflow triggers on push to main with path filters
scenarios.when: a push to main changes files under docs/**
scenarios.then: the workflow is triggered
and_then: changes to package.json, package-lock.json, and the workflow file also trigger it

scenarios.name: Workflow supports manual dispatch
scenarios.then: the workflow runs the full build and deploy pipeline

scenarios.name: Concurrency prevents simultaneous deploys
scenarios.when: multiple pushes happen in quick succession
scenarios.then: only the latest deployment runs (via concurrency group)

requirements.description: The workflow build job SHALL use Node 22, run npm ci and npm run docs:build, then upload the artifact via actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3
requirements.rationale: Ensures the build step produces and uploads the correct artifact for GitHub Pages deployment

scenarios.name: Build job uses Node 22
scenarios.then: actions/setup-node@v4 is configured with node-version: 22

scenarios.name: Build job installs dependencies with npm ci
scenarios.then: npm ci is used (not npm install) for deterministic builds

scenarios.name: Build artifact is uploaded from correct path
scenarios.then: actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3 uploads docs/.vitepress/dist

requirements.description: The workflow deploy job SHALL use actions/deploy-pages@v4 with github-pages environment
requirements.rationale: Deploys the built artifact to GitHub Pages following the standard pattern

scenarios.name: Deploy job depends on build
scenarios.then: it has needs: build
and_then: it only runs after the build job succeeds

scenarios.name: Deploy job sets github-pages environment
scenarios.then: it uses the github-pages environment

requirements.description: The .gitignore SHALL be updated to exclude node_modules/, docs/.vitepress/cache/, and docs/.vitepress/dist/
requirements.rationale: Prevents build artifacts and dependencies from being committed to the repository

scenarios.name: Node modules are ignored
scenarios.then: node_modules/ is not tracked by git

scenarios.name: VitePress cache is ignored
scenarios.then: docs/.vitepress/cache/ is not tracked by git

scenarios.name: VitePress dist is ignored
scenarios.then: docs/.vitepress/dist/ is not tracked by git

requirements.description: All new files SHALL carry SPDX Apache-2.0 license headers per the spdx-apache-20-header-on-infrastructure-files pattern
requirements.rationale: Maintains repository-wide license compliance with consistent header format

scenarios.name: TypeScript files have SPDX headers
scenarios.then: the file starts with // SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright 2026 SAP SE or an SAP affiliate company and // SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

scenarios.name: CSS file has SPDX header
scenarios.then: the file starts with /* SPDX-FileCopyrightText: ... */ and /* SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0 */

scenarios.name: Workflow file has SPDX header
scenarios.then: the file starts with # SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright 2026 SAP SE or an SAP affiliate company, blank comment, # SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

requirements.description: The workflow file SHALL use the .yaml extension per forge repository convention
requirements.rationale: All existing workflows in .github/workflows/ use .yaml extension; consistency is required

scenarios.name: Workflow uses .yaml extension
scenarios.then: it is named deploy-docs.yaml (not .yml)
and_then: the path filter in the workflow references the .yaml file

tasks.title: Create root-level package.json with VitePress devDependency and docs scripts (REQ-001)
tasks.description: Create /package.json with name 'c5c3-forge-docs', private: true, type: 'module', devDependencies: { vitepress: '^1.6.3' }, scripts: { 'docs:dev': 'vitepress dev docs', 'docs:build': 'vitepress build docs', 'docs:preview': 'vitepress preview docs' }. Mirror the architecture/package.json structure exactly.

tasks.title: Create docs/.vitepress/config.ts with site config, sidebar, and social link (REQ-002, REQ-009)
tasks.description: Create docs/.vitepress/config.ts with SPDX header (// comments), defineConfig with title 'CobaltCore Forge', base '/forge/', sidebar with Reference section containing 5 items linking to /reference/keystone-crd, /reference/ci-workflow, /reference/build-images-workflow, /reference/container-images, /reference/kubernetes-packages. Add socialLinks with GitHub icon linking to https://github.com/c5c3/forge. Follow the architecture/docs/.vitepress/config.ts pattern.

tasks.title: Create docs/.vitepress/theme/index.ts re-exporting DefaultTheme with custom.css (REQ-003, REQ-009)
tasks.description: Create docs/.vitepress/theme/index.ts identical to architecture/docs/.vitepress/theme/index.ts: SPDX header, import DefaultTheme from 'vitepress/theme', import './custom.css', export default DefaultTheme.

tasks.title: Create docs/.vitepress/theme/custom.css with wide-content styling (REQ-003, REQ-009)
tasks.description: Create docs/.vitepress/theme/custom.css identical to architecture/docs/.vitepress/theme/custom.css: SPDX header (/* */ comments), --vp-layout-max-width: 1440px, .VPDoc content container max-width overrides, code block overflow-x: auto, .VPSidebar width 280px (300px at >= 1440px), table overflow handling.

tasks.title: Create docs/index.md landing page with hero section and architecture docs link (REQ-004)
tasks.description: Create docs/index.md with VitePress frontmatter (layout: home), hero section (name: 'CobaltCore Forge', tagline describing the forge repository, actions linking to reference docs and architecture site at https://c5c3.github.io/C5C3/), and features section highlighting key areas (Reference Docs, Container Images, Kubernetes Operators).

tasks.title: Update .gitignore with VitePress and Node.js entries (REQ-008)
tasks.description: Append to .gitignore: a 'Node.js / VitePress' section header comment, then node_modules/, docs/.vitepress/cache/, docs/.vitepress/dist/ entries. Place after existing IDE section.

tasks.title: Create .github/workflows/deploy-docs.yaml with build and deploy jobs (REQ-005, REQ-006, REQ-007, REQ-009, REQ-010)
tasks.description: Create .github/workflows/deploy-docs.yaml following github-actions-workflow-spdx-header-and-structure-convention: SPDX header, --- separator, name 'Deploy Documentation', quoted 'on' with push (branches: [main], paths: docs/**, package.json, package-lock.json, .github/workflows/deploy-docs.yaml) and workflow_dispatch. Permissions: contents: read, pages: write, id-token: write. Concurrency group 'pages' with cancel-in-progress: false (matches reference pattern — only one deploy at a time). Build job: checkout@v4 with fetch-depth 0, setup-node@v4 with node-version 22 and cache npm, npm ci, npm run docs:build, configure-pages@v5, upload-pages-artifact@v3 with path docs/.vitepress/dist. Deploy job: environment github-pages, needs: build, deploy-pages@v4.

review_criteria: package.json matches specified structure: name 'c5c3-forge-docs', VitePress ^1.6.3 devDependency, all three scripts present
docs/.vitepress/config.ts has correct title, base path, all 5 sidebar entries with valid link paths, and GitHub social link
Theme and CSS files are identical in structure and content to architecture/docs/.vitepress/theme/ counterparts
docs/index.md has VitePress hero frontmatter, project description, and link to c5c3.github.io/C5C3
deploy-docs.yaml follows github-actions-workflow-spdx-header-and-structure-convention exactly: SPDX header, quoted on, permissions, concurrency, two-job structure
All new files (.ts, .css, .yaml) carry correct SPDX Apache-2.0 headers per spdx-apache-20-header-on-infrastructure-files pattern
.gitignore contains all three new entries: node_modules/, docs/.vitepress/cache/, docs/.vitepress/dist/
npm run docs:build succeeds without errors and produces valid static output
Workflow file uses .yaml extension (not .yml) and self-references with correct extension in paths filter
implementation_notes:
This feature creates a VitePress documentation site following the exact pattern established in the C5C3/C5C3 architecture repository (architecture/ submodule). Key architectural decisions:

1. **package.json at root**: Mirrors architecture/package.json structure (name, private, type module, VitePress devDep, three scripts). The forge repo has no existing root package.json.

2. **VitePress config**: Simpler sidebar than C5C3/C5C3 — only a flat 'Reference' section with 5 docs. The base path is '/forge/' (repo name) vs '/C5C3/' in the reference.

3. **Theme/CSS**: Identical to architecture/docs/.vitepress/theme/ — no customization needed beyond what C5C3/C5C3 already established.

4. **Workflow**: Based on architecture/.github/workflows/deploy-docs.yaml but adapted: (a) uses .yaml extension per forge convention (reference uses .yml), (b) concurrency group follows the reference pattern (group: pages, cancel-in-progress: false) rather than the forge CI pattern (group: ref-workflow, cancel-in-progress on PR). This is intentional — only one Pages deploy should ever run, matching the reference.

5. **Existing docs have frontmatter**: The 5 reference docs already have YAML frontmatter (title, quadrant, feature). VitePress will parse this natively — no modifications needed to existing files.

6. **No lockfile**: package-lock.json is generated by npm install, not authored manually. It should be committed after first npm install for reproducible CI builds (npm ci requires it).

Key files to reference:
- architecture/package.json — package.json template
- architecture/docs/.vitepress/config.ts — config.ts template (adapt sidebar)
- architecture/docs/.vitepress/theme/index.ts — theme/index.ts (copy exactly)
- architecture/docs/.vitepress/theme/custom.css — theme/custom.css (copy exactly)
- architecture/.github/workflows/deploy-docs.yaml — workflow template (adapt extension, paths)
- .github/workflows/ci.yaml — SPDX header and workflow convention reference

Risks:
- Reference docs have custom frontmatter (title, quadrant, feature) that VitePress will treat as page metadata. The 'title' field will be used as page title, which is the desired behavior.
- The workflow references package-lock.json in paths filter, but it won't exist until first npm install. This is acceptable — the workflow will be triggered by other path changes on the initial commit.
